west slope.


Assistant General Superintendent
II. A. Kennedy of the Great Northern
is in the Cascade range with small
army of bridge carpenters and labor
ers clearing the road of the big slides
of yesterday.


A special train was run from Spo-
kane last night with material and
bridge men to report to the general
superintendent at the Cascades.


Several bents in one bridge entire
ly wiped out and a large portion of
another destroyed by the heavy
slides. Passenger and freight traf-
flc is practically at a standstill.


The Everett & Monte Cristo line
sustained a heavy mudslide last
night. All coast lines have been
troubled with slides the last few
nights.


The S. +. & N. reports heavy SHOWS
